findings.message: record_raw_path stores the raw full path (including query string) as the dict key with no sanitize, no distinct-value cap, and no 'other' overflow bucket. Every distinct URL+querystring becomes a new key, so the backing store grows unboundedly. Unlike record_page_view (capped routes/attribution via _bump_capped) and the fixed-enum counters, this signal has unbounded cardinality and will blow up the /metrics series count.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize and bucket the value before storing: extract the route portion (strip the query string), run it through _san(), and bump via _bump_capped(self._raw_paths, route, self.MAX_ROUTES, overflow='other') so distinct values are capped with an 'other' overflow bucket.

findings.message: render_prometheus emits app_frontend_raw_path_total with one series per raw full path (path="..."), unsanitized and uncapped. This is an unbounded / high-cardinality label: every distinct full path (with query string) produces a separate Prometheus time series, which will overwhelm the scraper and TSDB. Every label here must be a fixed enum or pass through a distinct-value cap with an 'other' overflow, as the other counters in this file do.
findings.suggestion: Drop this raw-path counter, or first bound it at the ingest path (see record_raw_path) so the rendered series are limited to MAX_ROUTES sanitized routes plus an 'other' bucket.
